name: claude-sessions
description: Central sidebar for Claude Code activity: sessions across projects (with tokens/cost/subagents/projects-touched), KB file changes by day, and per-project file changes by day. Jump to resume, file, or diff.

view/item/context.when: view == claudeSessions && (viewItem == session || viewItem == sessionAutomated || viewItem == session-starred || viewItem == sessionAutomated-starred)

claudeSessions.limit.description: Maximum number of most-recent sessions to load.

claudeSessions.refresh.forceRecent.description: When the Refresh button is pressed, force a full re-parse of the N most recently-modified JSONLs (regardless of mtime cache). Catches on-disk edits — most notably session renames — that don't always bump mtime. Set to 0 to use only the cheap incremental sync.

claudeSessions.scriptPath.description: Path to session-center.sh (fallback when cacheEnabled is false).

claudeSessions.cacheEnabled.description: Use the SQLite cache. When true (default), the sidebar reads from a local DB and only re-parses JSONLs whose (mtime,size) changed. When false, falls back to spawning session-center.sh on every refresh (v0.6.x behavior).

claudeSessions.showAutomated.description: Show automated / cron-triggered sessions (entrypoint = sdk-cli, sdk, routine, etc.). Hidden by default since they're usually scheduled jobs (Google Doc digests, KB ingest, daily reports) rather than interactive work.

claudeSessions.filterByCurrentWorkspace.description: Show only sessions that originated from the current workspace's first folder (or a subfolder of it). Default on — keeps the sidebar focused on sessions you can actually resume from this window. Toggle off to see every session across every project.

claudeSessions.insightsLookbackDays.description: Insights dashboard: how many days of activity to chart.

claudeSessions.insightsDeepParse.description: Insights dashboard: how many most-recent sessions to parse deeply for thinking-time / tool-mix / burst-rate metrics. Higher = better stats, slower load.

claudeKbChanges.repoPath.description: Path to the git repo whose changes will be listed. Leave empty to auto-detect from the first workspace folder.

claudeKbChanges.lookbackDays.description: How many days of git history to load.

claudeProjectsActivity.repoPaths.description: Explicit list of project repos to watch. Auto-discovery is also applied (any ~/projects/* with commits in the lookback).

claudeProjectsActivity.autoDiscover.description: Walk ~/projects/ (depth 2) and include any git repo with commits in the lookback window.

claudeProjectsActivity.lookbackDays.description: How many days of git history to load per project.

claudeSessions.classify.backend.description: Where topic classification runs. `ollama` calls a local Ollama daemon (free, offline). `claude-p` invokes the Claude CLI in headless mode, using your subscription.

claudeSessions.liveStatusBar.enabled.description: Show a compact 'Claude · N active' indicator in the status bar. Polls every 5 s while sessions are active, 30 s when idle. Hover for a per-session breakdown; click to open the Live monitor.

claudeSessions.awaitingUser.notify.description: Pop a VS Code toast the first time a session enters an awaiting-user state (AskUserQuestion or ExitPlanMode). The status-bar item and Live monitor banner always highlight these; this only controls the one-shot toast.

claudeSessions.costBudget.daily.description: Daily Claude-Code budget in USD. When > 0, a status-bar tile shows today's spend vs this number; it turns amber at 80% and red at 100%. Set to 0 (default) to hide the tile.

claudeSessions.classify.autoOnOpen.description: When the conversation viewer opens and the session has unclassified turns, run topic classification in the background and refresh when done. Only fires when `classify.backend = ollama` to avoid burning paid `claude-p` tokens automatically.

claudeSessions.classify.autoBackground.description: Run topic classification continuously in the background: at extension startup the daemon scans for any session with unclassified turns and works through them one at a time, then re-scans on every sessions-view sync tick so new turns get picked up automatically. Per-turn cache means re-runs cost nothing once classified. Default backend is Ollama; see `classify.allowAutoBackgroundClaude` to opt into claude-p auto-runs.

claudeSessions.classify.allowAutoBackgro.description: Explicit opt-in: allow `classify.autoBackground` to run even when the backend is `claude-p`. Off by default so the daemon never quietly spends subscription tokens.

claudeSessions.classify.model.description: Model id passed to the chosen classification backend. For `ollama`, try `llama3.2:3b`, `qwen2.5:3b`, `gemma2:2b`. For `claude-p`, try `claude-haiku-4-5`.

claudeSessions.classify.batchSize.description: How many turns to classify per backend invocation. Smaller batches are more reliable with local Ollama models, which tend to drop ids when asked to echo back 20+ items. Drop to 5 if you still see 'N turns missing in response' errors.

claudeSessions.classify.claudeBin.description: Optional override for the `claude` CLI path. Only used when `classify.backend = claude-p`. Empty = use PATH.

claudeSessions.embedding.preferred.description: Which embedding backend to try first for the agent graph.

claudeSessions.embedding.ollamaUrl.description: Base URL for a local Ollama daemon.

claudeSessions.cluster.minPts.description: DBSCAN minPts for clustering sessions on the agent graph. With small corpora (<100 sessions) 3 finds clusters that 5 misses. If DBSCAN still finds none, the extension falls back to k-means.

claudeSessions.cluster.epsScale.description: DBSCAN eps as a fraction of the agent graph's axis range. Smaller = tighter clusters.

claudeSessions.trajectory.driftPercentil.description: Segments above this percentile of cosine distance between consecutive turns are flagged as topic drift in the trajectory view.

claudeTasks.showCrontab.description: Show the user's crontab as a section in the Tasks view. Clicking a row opens the crontab in an editor; saving installs it via `crontab <file>`.

claudeTasks.subagentLookbackMin.description: Tasks view: a Claude session counts as 'active sub-tasks running' if its JSONL was modified within this many minutes AND it currently has at least one running subagent. Set to 0 to disable the section.

scripts.package: npm run compile && npm run rebuild-native && vsce package --allow-missing-repository
